title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Recovery Case: Pathspecs
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git pathspecs increment in a revision-query and change-discovery workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.
Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Pathspecs
steps.instruction:
theory case — Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pathspecs contract for the supplied data.

content.text: Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. A defensible solution applies Pathspecs, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 5
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A shell glob and a Git pathspec always select the same paths. This fails the Pathspecs contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
